evidence: Near-identical runtime shape guards and coercion helpers are redefined in multiple modules (`toRecordOrEmpty`/`asString`/`asNumber` families in `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts:193-219`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts:13-27`, `src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts:237-260`, `src/shared/components/TaskDetails/VideoTravelDetails.tsx:20-52`).

Lightbox state is re-wrapped into multiple large intermediate objects (`lightboxCore`, `lightboxMedia`, `lightboxVariants`, `lightboxNavigation`) before passing into another adapter (`useLightboxStateValue`) in `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:180-279`.

Broad parameter bags with many optional branches are accepted and forwarded in task builders (`JoinClipsTaskParams` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts:59-110`, and orchestrator payload assembly in `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts:118-180`), increasing interface surface area without clear bounded sub-contracts.

Task-details seam duplicates translation logic that was already centralized in `taskParamsUtils`; `VideoTravelDetails` still performs its own fallback ladders for negative prompts, phase config, structure guidance, and arrays (`src/shared/components/TaskDetails/VideoTravelDetails.tsx:93-230`) instead of consuming one boundary-normalized model.

Query boundary filtering is duplicated between count and data queries in `useTasks` (`buildCountQuery` at `src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts:93-115` and `buildDataQuery` at `src/shared/hooks/useTasks.ts:117-162`), creating two integration seams that must stay manually in sync.

Video lightbox handoff requires a large nested object transfer into `useLightboxWorkflowProps` (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:357-400`), making cross-module contracts hard to reason about and easy to drift.

Several files are internally coherent but contain very long branch-heavy assembly functions with repeated optional-spread patterns, e.g. `buildTravelBetweenImagesPayload` (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts:118-315`) and `buildMagicEditTaskParams` (`src/shared/lib/tasks/magicEdit.ts:136-203`).

`useVideoLightboxControlsPanel` has an extremely large `useMemo` dependency list (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:97-142`), signaling brittle local internals and high accidental complexity for simple render branching.

`useSegmentSlotMode` combines URL deep-linking, pair selection, structure-video overlap mutation, and frame update orchestration in one hook (`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useSegmentSlotMode.ts:184-260` plus surrounding helpers), reducing local intent clarity.

findings.summary: Runtime task-param normalization is duplicated across builders and consumers, creating parallel abstractions with inconsistent contracts.
evidence: `individualTravelSegment.ts:193-251` defines local `toRecordOrEmpty/asNumber/asString/asBoolean/asPhaseConfig`.
`payloadBuilder.ts:13-27` redefines equivalent `toRecordOrEmpty/asNumber/asString`.
`taskParamsUtils.ts:237-268` defines another `asRecord/asString/toFiniteNumber/pickFirstString` stack.
`VideoTravelDetails.tsx:20-52` introduces yet another guard/coercion layer (`asRecordOrUndefined`, `asStringArray`, etc.).
findings.suggestion: Create one shared boundary-normalization module for task-param decoding (record guards, scalar coercion, array extraction, prompt/image derivation) and make task builders/details consume that typed normalized model instead of re-implementing guards per file.

findings.summary: Lightbox orchestration uses oversized state/prop bags across seams, increasing integration fragility and indirection cost.
evidence: `useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:180-279` builds multiple intermediate lightbox state objects before handing off to another composition hook.

`useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx:357-400` passes a deeply nested orchestration object into `useLightboxWorkflowProps` containing panel, shotWorkflow, actions, buttonGroupProps, refs, and segment mode data.
`ImageLightbox.tsx:68-88` plus `192-260` shows similarly broad grouped props (navigation/shotWorkflow/features/actions) and large handoff to shared state hook.
`LightboxLayout.tsx:37-76` rebuilds an additional model from multiple contexts and props, then fans into many child controls.
findings.suggestion: Introduce narrower seam contracts by splitting lightbox workflow into feature-specific adapters (navigation, variants, shot-actions, edit-mode) with smaller typed inputs; avoid one mega render-model object and one mega workflow call.

findings.summary: Task query filtering logic is duplicated for count and data paths, making boundary behavior easy to desynchronize.
evidence: `useTasks.ts:93-115` (`buildCountQuery`) and `117-162` (`buildDataQuery`) repeat the same project/status/taskType visibility filters with separate maintenance paths.
Both queries are executed together in `createPaginatedTasksQueryFn` (`useTasks.ts:218-223`), so any filter drift would silently produce inconsistent total counts vs returned rows.
Failures from either path route to the shared freshness manager (`useTasks.ts:225-234`), amplifying coupling between correctness of duplicated query seams and polling behavior.
findings.suggestion: Extract a single query-filter applicator used by both count and data builders (or construct one base filtered query and derive count/data variants), then keep ordering/range logic as the only divergence.
